title: Both root and $USER exist in rootless container
body:
I've been reading a lot of issues about mounting directories and user namespaces and so on, and I believe my use case is different enough from what is currently provided to open this ticket.

What I need in a rootless container are these 2 things.
* the entrypoint is (what it believes to be) root 
* I am $USER (as an example - uid 1000, gid 1000) both inside and outside the container so I can mount my $HOME and correctly use it.

The app that I work on runs as root and serves web pages that ultimately spawn process for non-root users. So those processes are owned by say uid 1000. So that's why I (uid 1000) don't want to **be** root, I want some room (0 mapped to, say, 100099). 

If there's some --uidmap magic that can accomplish this, please let me know, because I've tried just about all I can.

Also, as I'm writing this ticket I'm wondering if it's possible for a uid 100099 (that thinks it's root) that has uid 1000 as it's parent to spawn a process for that parent.  So if it's not possible or just plain too crazy for you to want to implement please let me know.

comments.body: Does `--userns=keep-id` work here? It preserves the UID of the user running the container in the mappings - UID 1000 on the host and in the container would be the same user. The container should still default to running applications as root.

When I'd tried that my app hung in weird places trying to escelate privileges. If i just `-it /bin/bash` into something like that and try to `sudo ls` it prompts for a password and I think that's causing issues - the fact that a shell script or similar has gets prompt.  A password I don't know btw, my actual host password doesn't seem to work.

I get that you may say 'well restructure/refactor your app to fit both' and that may be reasonable.

Hmm. I don't know if we expect sudo to work inside of rootless containers - at least, not natively...

@debarshiray Toolbox allows sudo, right? Do you know if you're doing anything special to allow that?
Yep, I think I see it [here](https://github.com/debarshiray/toolbox/blob/master/toolbox#L976). Let me try some stuff. Thanks!

Thanks you so much! That works just like a charm. 

FYI, just in case anyone in the future sees this, I added these lines to my build script and a simple `sudo -u root ....` to my entypoint. 
```shell
buildah run $ctr -- usermod -a -G wheel $USER
buildah run $ctr -- passwd --delete $USER
buildah run $ctr -- passwd --delete root
```
